vocab.word: a picture is worth a thousand words
vocab.ipa: /ə ˈpɪktʃər ɪz wɜːrθ ə ˈθaʊznd wɜːrdz/
vocab.meaning: một bức tranh có giá trị bằng nghìn lời nói
vocab.example: I realized early on that a picture is worth a thousand words, so I used drawing to express feelings I couldn't put into speech.

vocab.meaning: vơ đũa cả nắm / quy chụp (thường dùng để nói về việc học các kỹ năng nghệ thuật)
vocab.example: Not all children enjoy art, so we shouldn't paint them all with the same brush and assume everyone has a natural talent.

vocab.meaning: một tờ giấy trắng / sự khởi đầu hoàn toàn mới
vocab.example: I loved the feeling of staring at a blank canvas, knowing I had the freedom to create anything I wanted.

vocab.meaning: quay lại vạch xuất phát để làm lại từ đầu
vocab.example: Whenever my sketches didn't look right, I’d have to go back to the drawing board and start the anatomy from scratch.

vocab.meaning: tự làm khó mình / đưa mình vào thế bí
vocab.example: In my early painting lessons, I often painted myself into a corner by adding too many details too early in the process.

vocab.meaning: trống rỗng / không nghĩ ra được gì
vocab.example: There were days in art class when I would just draw a blank and couldn't decide what subject to put on paper.

vocab.meaning: nét vẽ cuối cùng / công đoạn hoàn tất cuối cùng
vocab.example: I remember the satisfaction of adding the finishing touch to a portrait after working on it for weeks.

vocab.meaning: phác thảo sơ bộ (ý tưởng hoặc hình ảnh)
vocab.example: My teacher taught me to always sketch out the basic shapes before committing to the final lines.

vocab.meaning: kỳ quặc, độc lạ, khác thường
vocab.example: My childhood drawings were a bit off the wall, featuring purple skies and flying houses, but my parents loved them.

vocab.meaning: kiệt tác (dùng để nói quá lên về tác phẩm thời thơ ấu)
vocab.example: I used to proudly present my finger paintings to my mum as if they were genuine masterpieces.
